title: Publisher Admission, Buffering, and Backpressure · Execution Trace: Drain Single Waiter
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must bound in-flight confirms and bytes, consume blocked signals, serialize channel use, and propagate saturation upstream; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the drain single waiter increment in a cancellable Go publisher admission controller. Learners progress…
objectives: Pause admission after false, coordinate a bounded drain waiter, handle close and abort, and avoid lost wakeups or listener leaks; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Treat a nil PublishWithContext error as local call acceptance only and separately track flow control, routing, confirmation, and stakeholder effect.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
